food_name: Teh-C (Singapore kopitiam Ceylon black tea with evaporated milk and sugar, hot)
aliases: Ceylon tea with evaporated milk Singapore
奶茶花奶 (Nǎi Chá Huā Nǎi)

english: Ceylon tea with evaporated milk Singapore
chinese: 奶茶花奶 (Nǎi Chá Huā Nǎi)

food_subgroup: Hot milk tea — Ceylon black tea brewed strong, served with evaporated milk (unsweetened, ~8% fat) and white sugar added separately

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic tea beverage class (FOODON_03306128). No specific entry exists for Teh-C (Singapore kopitiam Ceylon black tea with evaporated milk and sugar). Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Tea, black — used as closest parent for Teh-C (Singapore kopitiam Ceylon black tea with evaporated milk and sugar, hot)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0KZJ covers black tea as a category. No specific FoodEx2 entry exists for Teh-C as a prepared kopitiam beverage with evaporated milk and sugar.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Teh-C as a specific prepared beverage. SNOMED CT does not enumerate kopitiam beverage variants.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard kopitiam cup of Teh-C is approximately 220 ml (220 g), the conventional serving size at Singapore coffee shops.

nutrient_source.title: Teh-C (evaporated milk + sugar tea) — HPB Singapore (Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Teh-C (Ceylon black tea with evaporated milk and sugar, hot, standard kopitiam preparation). HPB Foodo is the primary approved regional source for Singapore dishes.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index Database — black tea with milk and sugar analogous estimate; Teh-C GI estimated at 65–72 based on evaporated milk and sugar composition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Teh-C in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 66 is estimated from the closest analogous food: tea with milk and sugar, where evaporated milk fat moderates glycemic response and lactose contributes a moderate GI component. The GI range of 65–72 is consistent with published values for sweetened milky tea beverages. GL per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (66 × 11.0g) / 100 ≈ 7.3, rounded to 7.2 per input source. Confidence 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (20 mg per 100 g; ~44 mg per 220 ml serving) and moderate caffeine from Ceylon tea (~50–75 mg per cup, lower than Robusta coffee)
hypertension.guidance: Teh-C is a suitable beverage choice for patients with hypertension due to its very low sodium content and lower caffeine load compared to kopitiam coffee; patients sensitive to caffeine should consider Teh-C Siu Dai (less sugar) or Teh-O Kosong as further reductions.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (GI 66) with low glycemic load per serving (GL ~7.2); sugar content approximately 8–12 g per cup, which is meaningfully lower than standard Teh (condensed milk tea)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Teh-C provides a clinically meaningful improvement over standard Teh for patients with Type 2 Diabetes, as the evaporated milk fat moderates glycemic response and the GL per serving remains low; ordering Teh-C Siu Dai (less sugar) further reduces the glycemic impact and is the preferred kopitiam tea option for glycaemic management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (1.1 g per 100 g; ~2.4 g per serving), low cholesterol (7 mg per 100 g), and theaflavins from Ceylon black tea with modest antioxidant benefit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Teh-C is acceptable for patients with cardiovascular disease in moderate consumption; the saturated fat contribution per serving is low, and Ceylon black tea theaflavins may offer modest cardioprotective benefit, though milk partially reduces theaflavin bioavailability; patients should limit added sugar by requesting Siu Dai.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Lower sugar content than standard Teh reduces pro-inflammatory load; theaflavins from Ceylon tea provide modest anti-inflammatory benefit
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Teh-C has a better anti-inflammatory profile than standard Teh due to lower sugar content and theaflavin content from Ceylon black tea; evaporated milk provides modest calcium (35 mg per 100 g) supporting bone health relevant to RA patients.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Teh-C is a classic Singapore kopitiam hot beverage consisting of strong Ceylon black tea brewed as a concentrate, served with unsweetened evaporated milk (approximately 8% fat, commonly the Carnation brand) and white sugar added separately by the customer or stall operator. It occupies the middle tier of the kopitiam tea matrix: richer and creamier than Teh-O (black tea with sugar only) due to the evaporated milk, but significantly less sweet than standard Teh (which uses sweetened condensed milk). Per 100 g, Teh-C provides approximately 36 kcal, 0.9 g protein, 1.7 g fat (1.1 g saturated), and 5.0 g carbohydrate (4.3 g sugar), with very low sodium (20 mg) and modest calcium (35 mg) from the evaporated milk. A standard kopitiam cup is approximately 220 ml. The glycemic index is estimated at 66 (medium), with a glycemic load of approximately 7.2 per serving — meaningfully lower than standard Teh. Clinically, Teh-C represents a practical upgrade for patients managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular risk who prefer milky tea: the evaporated milk fat moderates glycemic response, and the lower sugar content compared to condensed milk tea reduces overall glycaemic and caloric load. The kopitiam upgrade pathway for tea drinkers is: Teh → Teh Siu Dai → Teh-C → Teh-C Siu Dai → Teh-O Siu Dai → Teh-O Kosong. Ceylon black tea theaflavins provide modest antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefit, though bioavailability is partially reduced by milk binding. Teh-C is halal, vegetarian, and gluten-free.
meal_context: transitional kopitiam tea choice for health-conscious drinkers

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo, the primary approved regional source for Singapore kopitiam dishes. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework with documented basis. Ontology uses closest-parent codes with honest gap notes. Multilingual naming covers all five mandatory languages for Singaporean dishes.
notes: Dietary fibre is reported as 0.0 g per 100 g; this is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo — tea and evaporated milk contain no dietary fibre, and this is naturally zero for this beverage type. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 g per 100 g; confirmed zero per HPB Foodo source — evaporated milk from modern commercial production contains negligible industrial trans fat. GI value of 66 is an analogous estimate; no direct Teh-C entry exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GL per serving calculated at 7.2 based on approximately 11 g available carbohydrate per 220 g serving. The 'C' in Teh-C refers to 'Carnation' (the evaporated milk brand) in kopitiam tradition. Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat values are not reported by HPB Foodo for this entry and are set to -1. Water content not reported by source; set to -1.

Verified every entry in aliases_by_language appears verbatim in flat aliases. Confirmed 19-entry flat aliases list is a superset of all aliases_by_language entries.
